title: Ipomopsis havardii
taxonomy: Ipomopsis havardii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the evening primrose family, Onagraceae. It is native to North America and is commonly known as Havard's Ipomopsis. This plant is often placed in the genus Ipomopsis, which includes other species of evening primroses. Some sources group Ipomopsis havardii under the genus Camissonia, but the classification is not universally agreed upon. Further research may be needed to determine the most accurate classification for this species.

intro: Ipomopsis havardii, also known as Havard's ipomopsis or skyrocket, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its tall spikes of brightly colored flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Ipomopsis havardii include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being dried for use in traditional medicine blends.
